examples.title: Basic Digital Design Example - 2-to-1 Multiplexer
examples.description: Verilog code demonstrating a simple digital circuit at behavioral and structural levels
examples.explanation: This example shows two implementations of a 2-to-1 multiplexer: (1) Behavioral - describes functionality using if-else, synthesis tool decides gate implementation, (2) Structural - explicitly specifies gates. Both implement y = sel ? b : a. This demonstrates HDL abstraction levels.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - fixed number of gates regardless of input size

examples.title: RTL Description - Simple Register
examples.description: VHDL code showing Register-Transfer Level design with clock synchronization
examples.explanation: This VHDL code implements an 8-bit register with asynchronous reset at RTL. Key concepts: (1) Process block with sensitivity list (clk, rst), (2) rising_edge(clk) specifies synchronous operation, (3) if rst = '1' provides asynchronous reset, (4) Data transfers from input to output on clock edge - this is the essence of register-transfer level design.

examples.title: Digital Design Flow Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode illustrating the top-down digital design methodology
examples.explanation: This pseudocode represents the standard ASIC/FPGA design flow. Key stages: (1) Requirements analysis, (2) Architecture definition, (3) RTL design using HDLs, (4) Synthesis to gates with optimization, (5) Verification through simulation, (6) Physical design (place and route), (7) Timing analysis, (8) Fabrication. The loop-back arrows represent common design iterations due to verification failures or timing violations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n²) for synthesis and place-route on large designs
